This one's the bonus chapter — my closing thoughts after wrapping up the challenge.

I finally wrapped up another Ironman topic. And honestly, for my very first Ironman challenge, I went and took on two topics at once — one backend, one frontend. Mostly because I wanted to push myself to grow and soak up as much as I could, which is why I decided to
The whole thing played out exactly like what I said on the last day of my earlier ThinkPHP series:
the end is just another beginning
. Right after I wrapped this one up, my boss pulled me into yet another rabbit hole.
Just as I was thinking I could finally rest, my boss came to me one day and said this:
Boss: "Ray, I want to talk through some guidelines for the
service we're about to launch. So the example fields look like this, and the main goal is to keep the Ironman spirit and that dev energy alive."
Me: "I think we should add a few more fields, and do this and that..."
Boss: "Time to start recruiting people to write! Here are the participants so far!"
So then I went and DM'd my boss.
So... I guess I'll see you over at W3C HexSchool~ (Q@Q)
